name: Colcannon
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Head cabbage, quartered and core removed
2 lb Potatoes, scrubbed and sliced with skins left on
2 medium Leeks, thoroughly washed and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the cabbage until tender, about 12-15 minutes. Drain off the water and chop the cabbage. Set aside.
2. Bring another pot of water to a boil and cook the potatoes until tender. Drain off the water and set aside.
3. Put the leeks in a saucepan, cover with the milk, bring close to boiling and then turn down to a simmer until tender. Set aside.
4. Add the mace, salt, pepper, and garlic to the pot with the potatoes and mash well with a hand masher. Now add the leeks and their milk and mix in with the potatoes, taking care not to break down the leeks too much. If necessary, add a little more milk to make it smooth.
5. Add the chopped cabbage and continue mixing until well incorporated.
6. Mash in the butter until you achieve a smooth-buttery-potato texture with interesting pieces of leek and cabbage distributed throughout.
7. Transfer the mixture to an ovenproof dish, create a pattern on the surface, and place under the broiler to brown.
